reasoning: The Polymarket price was 0.20 on May 1. In mid-April 2025, reports revealed that Israel had planned to strike Iranian nuclear sites in May, but President Trump vetoed the operation to pursue direct nuclear negotiations with Iran. A second round of those talks was scheduled for May 10. For this market to resolve YES by June 30, the ongoing US-Iran talks would likely need to fail quickly, prompting Israel (or a US-Israeli joint operation) to instantly revert to military action. Iran has a strong incentive to prolong negotiations to avoid an attack, making a breakdown and subsequent strike within the next 50 days relatively unlikely. Given the time decay since May 1 and the active diplomatic process, the probability has decreased from the May 1 baseline of 20%, leading to an estimate of 15%.
